metadata.description: The official Ryu plugin marketplace — declarative, UI-less tools.

plugins.name: activity
plugins.description: The unified activity feed: everything happening on this node — monitor alerts, finished tasks, runs, approvals, meetings, and notes — newest first, grouped by day.

plugins.name: advisor
plugins.description: Consult a stronger reviewer model for a second opinion — as an auto-review turn hook (toggle / `/advisor`) and as the model-callable `advisor__consult` tool, a declarative HTTP tool routed through Core's `/api/advisor/consult` bridge.

plugins.name: approvals
plugins.description: Approval inbox: a human-in-the-loop queue where agent-proposed actions, edits, and fixes wait for your accept or reject before they take effect.

plugins.name: browser
plugins.description: A real-Chromium browser (Electron) Ryu runs as a local sidecar and exposes as the grant-gated `browser.control` capability: list/open/navigate tabs, screenshot, read titles, and (privileged) evaluate JS in a tab. Core spawns the sidecar lazily and idle-stops it; the desktop Browser panel drives it through the ext-proxy.

plugins.name: calendar
plugins.description: The scheduled-runs calendar: every agent and workflow scheduled job projected onto Month, Week, Day, and Agenda views — past runs from history, upcoming runs from cron/interval — with a New automation dialog that schedules an agent.

plugins.name: clips
plugins.description: Clips: capture and browse screen/timeline clips. A Core→Shadow proxy that depends on the Shadow capture app for its recordings. Runs the out-of-process ryu-clips sidecar; Core proxies /api/clips/* to it. The Shadow record/browse surface is fully served out-of-process (the sidecar reads RYU_SHADOW_URL itself); URL/yt-dlp ingest and auto-file into the Clips Space require Core's kernel machinery and degrade cleanly in the standalone sidecar.

plugins.name: dashboards
plugins.description: Dashboards: composable widget boards that assemble live views over monitors, meetings, quests, and other Core surfaces via soft HTTP loopback. Declares the out-of-process ryu-dashboards sidecar (kind:local) that owns the store + refresh loop + /api/dashboards/* surface, served to the desktop through the generic ext-proxy public_mount. Started eagerly (not lazy) so the kernel hardware device-dashboard renderer + nudge loop can reach it at boot / on a device poll before any desktop has opened Home. The dashboard_builder MCP runnable + the hardware render seam reach it over loopback via dashboards_client.

plugins.name: exa
plugins.description: Neural and keyword web search via the Exa API (https://exa.ai), exposed as two declarative HTTP tools: search and find-similar. Requires a BYOK Exa API key.

plugins.name: healing
plugins.description: Self-healing: failed runs are diagnosed by a Gateway side-model and proposed fixes are delivered to the approvals inbox for human review before they apply.

plugins.name: learning
plugins.description: Learning loop: turn chats and runs into reusable skills, gated by the approval Inbox, with an experience log of what was learned.

plugins.name: mail
plugins.description: Agent Inboxes — email as a service for agents. Runs the out-of-process ryu-mail sidecar; Core proxies /api/mail/* to it. Its sandboxed companion is a per-node mail client (list inboxes, read messages, compose/send, create an inbox) driving /api/mail/* via the mail:crud bridge capability.

plugins.name: meetings
plugins.description: Meeting notes: record → live transcript → AI notes, auto-saved into the Meetings Space so they stay editable and RAG-searchable.

plugins.name: monitors
plugins.description: Website monitors: price, stock, keyword, content, and uptime watches with cross-device notification fan-out.

plugins.name: quests
plugins.description: Quests: auto-detecting todos surfaced from your chats and activity, tracked as a lightweight quest board with progress and completion.

plugins.name: recipes
plugins.description: Desktop-automation recipes: record → save → replay UI action sequences. Backed by Ghost's RecipeStore, so it declares Ghost as a dependency and the graph refuses to disable Ghost out from under it.

plugins.name: research
plugins.description: Deep research: multi-step web research runs backed by the autoresearch sidecar, with sources, findings, and RAG-searchable reports. Declares the out-of-process ryu-research sidecar (a thin Rust proxy that forwards /api/research/* to the Python autoresearch engine on :8087); Core reaches it via the generic ext-proxy loader.

plugins.name: rtk
plugins.tagline: Token-compress noisy dev-command output for agents
plugins.description: Run a dev command through RTK (Rust Token Killer) and get a token-compressed version of its output (60-90% fewer tokens), exposed as a declarative `command` tool that shells out to the locally-installed `rtk` binary. BYO — no API key.

plugins.name: sample-widget
plugins.description: Reference third-party MCP widget plugin. A tiny local Node MCP server (server.mjs) exposes one render tool (sample_widget__render) that returns structured data AND serves a self-contained skybridge HTML resource (ui://widget/sample.html). contributes.widgets binds the tool to that resource; the widget:render grant + this manifest are the consent gate that lets Core promote the tool's result into an inline chat widget. Copy this directory as the starting point for your own widget plugin.

plugins.name: shadow
plugins.tagline: Recall what you have seen and done
plugins.description: Search everything Shadow has captured (screen text, audio transcripts, input) and summarize recent activity. Exposed as declarative HTTP tools that reach the device-local Shadow sidecar through Core's authenticated `/api/shadow/*` proxy (which injects Shadow's bearer token).

plugins.name: simulator
plugins.tagline: iOS Simulator & Android Emulator control for agents
plugins.description: Drive iOS Simulators (macOS + Xcode) and Android Emulators from a workspace tab. Ryu runs the device-control toolchain (`simctl`, `adb`) as a local sidecar and exposes the grant-gated `simulator.control` capability: list/boot devices, install & launch apps, open deep links, screenshot, and (Android) tap/swipe/type. The desktop Simulator panel shows whichever platform the connected node supports.

plugins.name: skill-editor
plugins.description: Author a user-owned Agent Skill (SKILL.md): front-matter fields (name / description / allowed tools / always-on), a markdown body editor, debounced autosave, and server-backed undoable version history.

plugins.name: spider
plugins.tagline: Crawl and extract web pages for agents
plugins.description: High-performance web crawling and content extraction via the Spider CLI (https://spider.cloud), exposed as a declarative `command` tool that shells out to the locally-installed `spider` binary. No API key.

plugins.name: teams
plugins.description: Teams: named groups of agents you can address as one. Governance shell over the in-crate teams store (agent-id membership only); no documents, no external dependencies. Runs the out-of-process ryu-teams sidecar; Core proxies /api/teams/* to it.

plugins.name: timeline
plugins.description: The activity replay timeline: a CapCut-style scrubber over Shadow's captured screen/input/window/audio lanes with a keyframe preview, a Dayflow work journal, and jump-to-moment focus.

plugins.name: voice
plugins.description: Voice data path: speech-to-text transcription (whisper.cpp) and text-to-speech (OuteTTS + the universal Ryu TTS sidecar), plus the installable TTS model catalog. Governance shell over the in-crate voice module.

plugins.name: webhooks
plugins.description: Inbound webhook endpoint registry: resolved public URLs, secret presence, last-delivery times, and the ingress backend status.

plugins.name: workflows
plugins.description: Workflows: petgraph DAG automation with triggers, durable execution, and a natural-language workflow builder.
